news.headline: Archbishop Sample responds to Gov. Kotek's Abortion Provider Appreciation Day with Pastoral Teaching letter.
news.summary: On March 10, 2025, Governor Tina Kotek of Oregon proclaimed Abortion Provider Appreciation Day, prompting Archbishop Alexander Sample to release a Pastoral Teaching letter in Portland, Oregon. The Archbishop expressed profound sorrow and spiritual concerns over the proclamation. The event gained attention in the area with significant media coverage and discussions among the public on the intersection of government proclamations and religious beliefs.

DigiFT launches Invesco’s $6.3B private credit token on Arbitrum, enhancing RWA utility for institutions and DeFi apps. #DeFi #Tokenization
news.summary: DigiFT digital asset exchange has unveiled Invesco's tokenized private credit strategy on Ethereum layer-2 network Arbitrum, expanding real-world assets' use cases for institutional investors. The iSNR token, tracking Invesco's private credit fund with $6.3 billion AUM, was launched on Feb 19, requiring a minimum investment of $10,000. DigiFT CEO Henry Zhang noted the increased utility of this integration for DeFi apps and institutional investors. The RWA tokenization trend is on the rise despite recent market downturns, with Securitize also introducing tokenized products and Franklin Templeton launching tokenized money funds. Industry data reveal RWAs onchain have grown by 17.5% to $18.1 billion over the past month, with private credit and US Treasury debt making up 91% of this total ($12.2 billion and $4.2 billion, respectively).

news.headline: Senate Banking Committee voted 18-6 to advance GENIUS stablecoin bill. Warren's amendments rejected. Bill aims to regulate stablecoins.
news.summary: The United States Senate Banking Committee voted 18-6 to advance the GENIUS Act, a bill aimed at regulating stablecoins. Senator Elizabeth Warren's proposed amendments, including limiting stablecoin issuance to banking institutions, were not included in the bill. Senator Tim Scott, chairman of the committee, praised the bill for promoting innovation and protecting consumers, with provisions such as one-to-one reserves and anti-money laundering compliance. The bill must still pass a vote in both chambers of Congress before reaching President Trump. Senator Bill Hagerty defended the bill against Warren's amendments, highlighting existing consumer protection, AML provisions, and recently added stricter reserve requirements. The bill is seen as a step towards merging stablecoins with the traditional financial system, with the Trump administration looking to leverage stablecoins to protect the US dollar's global reserve status. The industry views the bill as a positive development, with US-based firms expected to gain a competitive edge due to the stricter provisions.

news.summary: Discover the latest insights on how individuals in the millennial generation are choosing to invest their money and prepare for retirement. news.headline: Billionaire Dan Loeb exits Bath & Body Works stake, had 11,785,000 shares. Sales declined 4.3% to $2.788 billion in Q4 2024.
news.summary: In the fourth quarter of 2024, billionaire Dan S. Loeb, founder of Third Point, completely sold off his stake in Bath & Body Works, Inc. (NYSE: BBWI). His holdings in the company stood at 11,785,000 shares as of the third quarter of 2024. Over recent quarters, Loeb had been progressively reducing his stake in Bath & Body Works, having previously held 11,975,000 shares in Q2 2024, 12,850,000 shares in Q1 2024, and 13,850,000 shares in Q4 2023.After announcing a fourth-quarter sales dip of 4.3% year-on-year, totaling $2.788 billion, in February, the company surpassed expectations with adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $2.09 compared to an estimated $2.05. Bath & Body Works also issued guidance for FY25. For more details, visit a reliable news source.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs predicts a 50-100% surge in U.S. copper imports due to potential tariffs, adding 200,000-300,000 metric tons by Q3. Supply deficit expected in 2025.
news.summary: Goldman Sachs predicts a sharp increase in U.S. copper imports due to anticipated tariffs from the Trump administration. The investment bank forecasts a 25% duty on copper imports by the end of the year, prompting a surge in stockpiling to preempt the tariff implementation. This could lead to a 50-100% rise in U.S. net copper imports, adding 200,000 to 300,000 metric tons to domestic inventories by Q3. If this scenario plays out, U.S. copper stockpiles may soar from 95,000 tons to potentially 400,000 tons, comprising about half of global reported inventories and leaving the global market with historically low inventory levels. Looking ahead to 2025, Goldman Sachs anticipates a global copper deficit of 180,000 tons, driven by increased demand for electrification, economic stimulus initiatives in China, and slow growth in mine production. The bank expects the supply-demand imbalance to be more pronounced in the latter half of the year.

news.headline: Nasdaq 100 drops 13% from Feb. 18, 2025 peak, now 4% below 200-day moving avg. Analysts suggest it may be a buying opportunity. Economic concerns and tariff worries cited. Some predict potential for a bear market.
news.summary: The Nasdaq 100 index has dropped 13% from its record highs in February 2025 and is now 4% below its 200-day moving average, causing concerns among investors. The sell-off in big tech stocks is attributed to worries about tariffs, slowing economic growth, and inflation. The Atlanta Fed predicts a 2.4% economic decline in Q1 2025, while Goldman Sachs lowered its GDP forecast to 1.7% due to anticipated higher tariffs. Some experts, like Ed Yardeni, believe the market might be in a bear market since February 20, potentially leading to a recession and wider budget deficit. However, not all analysts share this view.

news.headline: Senators criticize Pfizer's DTC model with UpScriptHealth, questioning financial ties. Four senators pen letter; concerns raised on PfizerForAll directing patients to drugs, potentially leading to unnecessary prescriptions and higher costs.
news.summary: In August 2024, Pfizer Inc (NYSE:PFE) launched the PfizerForAll digital platform in partnership with UpScriptHealth, Alto Pharmacy, and Instacart. A letter sent to UpScriptHealth CEO Peter Ax by four senators - Richard Durbin, Elizabeth Warren, Peter Welch, and Bernie Sanders - raised concerns about the financial ties between Pfizer and UpScriptHealth, questioning potential violations of the federal Anti-Kickback Statute (AKS). The letter mentioned that PfizerForAll, backed by Pfizer, appeared to steer patients towards specific medications, potentially resulting in unnecessary prescriptions and increased expenses. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Gold reaches record high while Bitcoin underperforms, with BTC at $80,000 after briefly touching $85,000. Fed Chair Powell's comments contribute to BTC's struggles as he dismisses it as a true currency.
news.summary: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is currently underperforming compared to gold, with the latter reaching a record high on Thursday. This has raised doubts about Bitcoin's portrayal as 'digital gold'. In December 2024, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell spoke at the DealBook Summit, likening Bitcoin to a speculative asset akin to gold but in digital form. Powell discredited Bitcoin as a legitimate currency due to its high volatility and limited acceptance for payments or as a stable store of value. Since his statements, the disparity between Bitcoin and gold has increased. Bitcoin experienced a brief surge, reaching $85,000 before retracting to $80,000. This trend is discussed further in the full article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Ripple-SEC lawsuit over $1.3B XRP sale nears end. Focus on whether XRP will be classified as a commodity like Ethereum.
news.summary: After a lengthy legal dispute starting in December 2020, the SEC and Ripple Labs seem close to resolving the matter regarding the alleged $1.3 billion unregistered XRP sale. The focus of the negotiations lies in determining if XRP's utility and trading nature are more akin to a commodity, like Ethereum (ETH), rather than a security. This comparison is pivotal in shaping the case's outcome as the SEC reviews its position with new leadership. Charles Gasparino, a senior correspondent at Fox Business, highlighted Ethereum's treatment as a commodity as a crucial aspect of the talks. Gasparino indicated that Ethereum's example plays a key role in the discussions.

news.headline: Wall Street plunges as trade war fears heighten, S&P 500 hits 7-month low; Nasdaq 100 slides 0.6% midday.
news.summary: Wall Street faced further losses on Tuesday due to ongoing trade war fears. The Nasdaq 100 fell 0.6% at midday, with the S&P 500 hitting a seven-month low. Only 44% of S&P 500 stocks are trading above their 200-day moving average, the lowest since November 2023, indicating broad market weakness. The Dow Jones Industrial Average underperformed, particularly in industrial stocks, as trade tensions escalated. President Trump heightened these tensions by raising tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum imports to 50% in response to Ontario's tariffs on electricity exports. This tariff increase will take effect on Wednesday, adding to market uncertainty.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs analyst initiates coverage on agriculture stocks, predicts sector growth post Russia-Ukraine war peak.Key picks: Corteva (NYSE: CTVA) with Buy rating and $71 price target, highlighting strong business and cost advantages.Projected growth fueled by higher corn acreage and favorable market conditions.
news.summary: An analyst from Goldman Sachs, Duffy Fischer, has recently begun coverage on several prominent U.S. agriculture input stocks. Fischer believes that the agricultural macro environment is currently in the process of stabilizing following the peak due to the Russia-Ukraine conflict. Looking ahead to Spring 2025, Fischer predicts a more favorable outlook than initially expected, especially with projected increases in corn acreage and a limited Fall application window. However, Fischer foresees a gradual return to normalized levels later in the year. Specifically focusing on Corteva, Inc. (NYSE: CTVA), Fischer has given the company a Buy rating and set a price target of $71. The analyst underscores Corteva's robust seed and crop protection segments, which are anticipated to gain from substantial cost advantages in 2025. For further details, the complete story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: This week, President Trump caused turmoil in the markets by imposing new tariffs on Canada. The announcement led to a sharp decline in stock prices, highlighting that the previously stable stock market is no longer a reliable investment option for investors.

news.headline: Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege X, a major update with new mode Dual Front, launches June 10 on multiple platforms.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, Ubisoft revealed that Tom Clancy’s Rainbow Six Siege® X, the biggest advancement in the game's development, is set to release as a complimentary update on June 10. The update will be available on various platforms including Ubisoft+, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X | S, Windows PC (Steam), and Amazon Luna. The update will feature the new Dual Front mode, a 6v6 multiplayer mode that will continually change and is a permanent addition to the game. Players will need to secure three objectives in this mode.

news.headline: Healthcare sector faces cyber wake-up call post Change Healthcare/UHC breach, Info-Tech Research releases critical insights.
news.summary: Following the recent data breach involving Change Healthcare/UHC, significant weaknesses in the healthcare industry have come to light, such as outdated technology and inadequate security protocols. In light of this, Info-Tech Research Group, a renowned global IT research and advisory firm, has released fresh findings to address these concerns. This breach has affected a large number of individuals' sensitive information and highlights the urgent need for improved cybersecurity measures within the healthcare sector.

news.headline: Data breach at Erickson Companies, LLC detected on Nov 18, 2024, affecting customer and employee records with personal info.
news.summary: In Chandler, Arizona, Erickson Companies, LLC discovered irregular activity in its network on November 18, 2024. By February 19, 2025, it was confirmed that unauthorized individuals may have accessed records of customers, employees, and dependents/beneficiaries. The compromised data includes names, addresses, Social Security numbers, and driver’s license numbers. This notification is pertinent to those associated with Erickson, urging vigilance and precaution following this security breach.

The protest underscores the urgency for a resolution, as the negotiations affect a significant number of employees and the industry's operations. The technicians' call for action emphasizes the need for fair working conditions and a swift resolution to the ongoing contract discussions.

news.headline: Northwest drivers prioritize saving money over environment in switch to EVs; 66% likely to switch, with 44% motivated by gas cost reduction.
news.summary: A recent poll by PEMCO Mutual Insurance in Washington and Oregon revealed that only 1 in 10 drivers currently own an electric vehicle (EV), but over two-thirds are considering switching to an electric or hybrid vehicle for their next purchase. The main driver behind this interest is not necessarily environmental concerns, with 44% of respondents citing cost savings on gas as their primary reason for considering an EV. Additionally, 12% mentioned tax credits as an influential factor, while only 38% mentioned reducing carbon emissions as a top motivator. This marks a shift from 2021 when 54% of drivers prioritized lowering emissions. Despite the growing interest in EVs, challenges remain, with nearly half of the respondents expressing concerns about integrating an electric vehicle into their lifestyle.
